title: Fish and seafood production
subtitle: Fish and seafood production is measured as the sum of seafood from wild catch and fish farming (aquaculture).
caption:
Fish and seafood production

Fish and seafood production is measured as the sum of seafood from wild catch and fish farming (aquaculture).

This choropleth world map displays 2023 fish and seafood production volumes by country, measured in tonnes. The legend ranges from 0 tonnes to over 30 million tonnes, with specific thresholds at 100,000, 300,000, 1 million, 3 million, and 10 million tonnes. China sits alone in the darkest blue band, indicating production exceeding 30 million tonnes. Indonesia and India fall into the next tier, appearing in dark blue shades that correspond to the 10 million to 30 million tonne range. The United States, Russia, and Peru occupy the mid-to-dark blue bands, placing them between 3 million and 10 million tonnes. A broader group of nations, including Japan, Chile, Norway, and Vietnam, cluster in the medium blue shades representing 1 million to 3 million tonnes. Much of South America, including Brazil and Argentina, along with countries in Southern Africa and Australia, appear in lighter teal and green shades, indicating production levels between 100,000 and 1 million tonnes. Large portions of Central Africa, the Middle East, and Central Asia are colored in the lightest yellow shades, signifying production below 100,000 tonnes. Greenland and several smaller island territories display a hatched pattern, denoting no available data. The visual distribution highlights a concentration of high-volume production in East and Southeast Asia, with moderate levels scattered across the Americas and Europe, and lower volumes prevalent across the African continent.
